2605.05386.title: BALAR : A Bayesian Agentic Loop for Active Reasoning
2605.05386.paper_summary: Large language models increasingly operate in interactive settings where solving a task requires multiple rounds of information exchange with a user. However, most current systems treat dialogue reactively and lack a principled mechanism to reason about what information is missing and which question should be asked next. We propose BALAR (Bayesian Agentic Loop for Active Reasoning), a task-agnostic outer-loop algorithm that requires no fine-tuning and enables structured multi-turn interaction between an LLM agent and a user. BALAR maintains a structured belief over latent states, selects clarifying questions by maximizing expected mutual information, and dynamically expands its state representation when the current one proves insufficient. We evaluate BALAR on three diverse benchmarks: AR-Bench-DC (detective cases), AR-Bench-SP (thinking puzzles), and iCraft-MD (clinical diagnosis). BALAR significantly outperforms all baselines across all three benchmarks, with $14.6\%$ higher accuracy on AR-Bench-DC, $38.5\%$ on AR-Bench-SP, and $30.5\%$ on iCraft-MD.
2605.05386.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地在互動環境中運作，解決任務需要與用戶進行多輪信息交流。<lb/>然而，目前大多數系統都是被動地處理對話，缺乏一個原則性的機制來推理缺少了哪些信息以及應該接下來問哪個問題。<lb/>我們提出了 BALAR（Bayesian Agentic Loop for Active Reasoning），這是一個與任務無關的外部循環算法，無需微調，並能夠實現 LLM 代理和用戶之間的結構化多輪互動。<lb/>BALAR 維持對潛在狀態的結構化信念，通過最大化期望互信息來選擇澄清問題，並在當前狀態不足時動態擴展其狀態表示。<lb/>我們在三個不同的基準上評估了 BALAR：AR-Bench-DC（偵探案例）、AR-Bench-SP（思維謎題）和 iCraft-MD（臨床診斷）。<lb/>BALAR 在所有三個基準上顯著超越了所有基準線，在 AR-Bench-DC 上準確率提高了 $14.6\%$，在 AR-Bench-SP 上提高了 $38.5\%$，在 iCraft-MD 上提高了 $30.5\%$。
2605.05386.author: Aymen Echarghaoui et.al.
2605.05386.authors: Aymen Echarghaoui, Dongxia Wu, Emily B. Fox
